[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/consolidation-labels.md","old_string":"Learn plan consolidation has a circular reference problem: `/local:replan-learn-plans` queries all open `erk-learn` issues and merges them into a single new issue — which itself carries the `erk-learn` label. Without protection, the next consolidation run would pick up its own output, creating an infinite loop of consolidation.

The `erk-consolidated` label breaks this cycle. It acts as a "already processed" marker: the consolidation query fetches `erk-learn` issues, then filters out any that also carry `erk-consolidated`.","new_string":"Learn plan consolidation has a circular reference problem: `/local:replan-learn-plans` queries all open `erk-learn` plans and merges them into a single new plan — which itself carries the `erk-learn` label. Without protection, the next consolidation run would pick up its own output, creating an infinite loop of consolidation.

The `erk-consolidated` label breaks this cycle. It acts as a "already processed" marker: the consolidation query fetches `erk-learn` plans, then filters out any that also carry `erk-consolidated`."}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/consolidation-labels.md","old_string":"- **Multi-plan consolidation** creates a new issue that _replaces_ multiple source issues. It must not be re-consolidated because it already represents a merge.
- **Single-plan replan** creates a new issue that _supersedes_ one source issue. It's a fresh plan against current codebase state and should remain eligible for future consolidation with other plans.","new_string":"- **Multi-plan consolidation** creates a new plan that _replaces_ multiple source plans. It must not be re-consolidated because it already represents a merge.
- **Single-plan replan** creates a new plan that _supersedes_ one source plan. It's a fresh plan against current codebase state and should remain eligible for future consolidation with other plans."}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/consolidation-labels.md","old_string":"The label application happens in Step 7 of `/erk:replan`, after the new plan issue is created via `/erk:plan-save`.","new_string":"The label application happens in Step 7 of `/erk:replan`, after the new plan is created via `/erk:plan-save`."}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/consolidation-labels.md","old_string":"The filtering happens early in `/local:replan-learn-plans` (Step 1b) to avoid wasting API calls investigating issues that will be skipped.","new_string":"The filtering happens early in `/local:replan-learn-plans` (Step 1b) to avoid wasting API calls investigating plans that will be skipped."}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/consolidation-labels.md","old_string":"An issue accumulates labels through its lifecycle — `erk-consolidated` is additive, not a replacement:

1. `/erk:learn` creates issue → labeled `erk-learn`
2. `/local:replan-learn-plans` consolidates N issues → new issue labeled `erk-learn` + `erk-consolidated` + `erk-plan`
3. Original N issues are closed with a cross-reference comment
4. Future consolidation runs skip the `erk-consolidated` issue automatically

The `erk-plan` label is added during consolidation (Step 7.4 of `/erk:replan`) to make consolidated plans dispatchable via `erk pr dispatch`, which requires this label.","new_string":"A plan accumulates labels through its lifecycle — `erk-consolidated` is additive, not a replacement:

1. `/erk:learn` creates plan → labeled `erk-learn`
2. `/local:replan-learn-plans` consolidates N plans → new plan labeled `erk-learn` + `erk-consolidated` + `erk-plan`
3. Original N plans are closed with a cross-reference comment
4. Future consolidation runs skip the `erk-consolidated` plan automatically

The `erk-plan` label is added during consolidation (Step 7.4 of `/erk:replan`) to make consolidated plans dispatchable via `erk pr dispatch`, which requires this label."}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/consolidation-labels.md","old_string":"## Edge Case: All Issues Already Consolidated

When `/local:replan-learn-plans` finds open `erk-learn` issues but every one has `erk-consolidated`, it reports this state and stops — there's nothing new to consolidate. This is a normal steady-state condition after a consolidation has run and no new learn plans have been created since.","new_string":"## Edge Case: All Plans Already Consolidated

When `/local:replan-learn-plans` finds open `erk-learn` plans but every one has `erk-consolidated`, it reports this state and stops — there's nothing new to consolidate. This is a normal steady-state condition after a consolidation has run and no new learn plans have been created since."}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/consolidation-labels.md","old_string":"## Edge Case: All Issues Already Consolidated

When `/local:replan-learn-plans` finds open `erk-learn` issues but every one has `erk-consolidated`, it reports this state and stops — there's nothing new to consolidate. This is a normal steady-state condition after a consolidation has run and no new learn plans have been created since.","new_string":"## Edge Case: All Plans Already Consolidated

When `/local:replan-learn-plans` finds open `erk-learn` plans but every one has `erk-consolidated`, it reports this state and stops — there's nothing new to consolidate. This is a normal steady-state condition after a consolidation has run and no new learn plans have been created since."}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/cross-artifact-analysis.md","old_string":"| Relationship | Description                                 | Detection Method                            |
| ------------ | ------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| Linked       | PR explicitly references plan issue         | PR body contains `Plan: #<issue>` reference |
| Superseding  | PR implements work that makes plan obsolete | Compare PR changes with plan intent         |
| Partial      | PR implements subset of plan scope          | Evidence-based analysis of overlap          |
| Independent  | PR and plan address unrelated concerns      | No meaningful overlap in changes            |","new_string":"| Relationship | Description                                 | Detection Method                            |
| ------------ | ------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| Linked       | PR explicitly references plan              | PR body contains `Plan: #<number>` reference |
| Superseding  | PR implements work that makes plan obsolete | Compare PR changes with plan intent         |
| Partial      | PR implements subset of plan scope          | Evidence-based analysis of overlap          |
| Independent  | PR and plan address unrelated concerns      | No meaningful overlap in changes            |"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/cross-artifact-analysis.md","old_string":"| Relationship | Description                                 | Detection Method                            |
| ------------ | ------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| Linked       | PR explicitly references plan issue         | PR body contains `Plan: #<issue>` reference |
| Superseding  | PR implements work that makes plan obsolete | Compare PR changes with plan intent         |
| Partial      | PR implements subset of plan scope          | Evidence-based analysis of overlap          |
| Independent  | PR and plan address unrelated concerns      | No meaningful overlap in changes            |","new_string":"| Relationship | Description                                 | Detection Method                            |
| ------------ | ------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| Linked       | PR explicitly references plan              | PR body contains `Plan: #<number>` reference |
| Superseding  | PR implements work that makes plan obsolete | Compare PR changes with plan intent         |
| Partial      | PR implements subset of plan scope          | Evidence-based analysis of overlap          |
| Independent  | PR and plan address unrelated concerns      | No meaningful overlap in changes            |"}

... [149 lines omitted] ...

[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/learn-plan-metadata-fields.md","old_string":"This field enables:

- Debugging failed plan creations by linking to the workflow run
- Tracing provenance of automatically generated plans
- Quick navigation from plan issue to the workflow that created it","new_string":"This field enables:

- Debugging failed plan creations by linking to the workflow run
- Tracing provenance of automatically generated plans
- Quick navigation from plan to the workflow that created it"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/learn-plan-validation.md","old_string":"The `/erk:learn` skill performs the authoritative check in its Step 1 by fetching the issue via `erk exec get-issue-body` and inspecting the `labels` array. If `erk-learn` is present, the skill halts with a cycle prevention error.","new_string":"The `/erk:learn` skill performs the authoritative check in its Step 1 by fetching the plan via `erk exec get-issue-body` and inspecting the `labels` array. If `erk-learn` is present, the skill halts with a cycle prevention error."}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/learn-plan-validation.md","old_string":"The `/erk:learn` skill performs the authoritative check in its Step 1 by fetching the issue via `erk exec get-issue-body` and inspecting the `labels` array. If `erk-learn` is present, the skill halts with a cycle prevention error.","new_string":"The `/erk:learn` skill performs the authoritative check in its Step 1 by fetching the plan via `erk exec get-issue-body` and inspecting the `labels` array. If `erk-learn` is present, the skill halts with a cycle prevention error."}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/plan-backend-migration.md","old_string":"## PlanBackend Methods

Key methods on `PlanBackend` ABC (`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/backend.py`):

| Method                                             | Returns                  | Description                                 |
| -------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------- |
| `get_plan(repo_root, plan_id)`                     | `Plan \| PlanNotFound`   | Fetch full plan by ID                       |
| `get_metadata_field(repo_root, plan_id, field)`    | `object \| PlanNotFound` | Get a single metadata field value           |
| `update_metadata(repo_root, plan_id, metadata)`    | `None \| PlanNotFound`   | Update metadata fields in plan header block |
| `add_label(repo_root, plan_id, label)`             | `None` (raises on fail)  | Add a label to a plan                       |
| `post_event(repo_root, plan_id, event_type, data)` | `None`                   | Post a lifecycle event to the plan          |

`add_label()` raises `RuntimeError` if the provider fails or the plan is not found (unlike `get_plan`/`update_metadata` which use the `PlanNotFound` result type).","new_string":"## PlanBackend Methods

Key methods on `PlanBackend` ABC (`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/backend.py`):

| Method                                             | Returns                  | Description                                 |
| -------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------- |
| `get_plan(repo_root, plan_id)`                     | `Plan \| PlanNotFound`   | Fetch full plan by ID                       |
| `get_metadata_field(repo_root, plan_id, field)`    | `object \| PlanNotFound` | Get a single metadata field value           |
| `update_metadata(repo_root, plan_id, metadata)`    | `None \| PlanNotFound`   | Update metadata fields in plan header block |
| `add_label(repo_root, plan_id, label)`             | `None` (raises on fail)  | Add a label to a plan                       |
| `post_event(repo_root, plan_id, event_type, data)` | `None`                   | Post a lifecycle event to the plan          |

`add_label()` raises `RuntimeError` if the provider fails or the plan is not found (unlike `get_plan`/`update_metadata` which use the `PlanNotFound` result type)."}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/plan-backend-migration.md","old_string":"## PlanBackend Methods

Key methods on `PlanBackend` ABC (`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/backend.py`):

| Method                                             | Returns                  | Description                                 |
| -------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------- |
| `get_plan(repo_root, plan_id)`                     | `Plan \| PlanNotFound`   | Fetch full plan by ID                       |
| `get_metadata_field(repo_root, plan_id, field)`    | `object \| PlanNotFound` | Get a single metadata field value           |
| `update_metadata(repo_root, plan_id, metadata)`    | `None \| PlanNotFound`   | Update metadata fields in plan header block |
| `add_label(repo_root, plan_id, label)`             | `None` (raises on fail)  | Add a label to a plan                       |
| `post_event(repo_root, plan_id, event_type, data)` | `None`                   | Post a lifecycle event to the plan          |

`add_label()` raises `RuntimeError` if the provider fails or the plan is not found (unlike `get_plan`/`update_metadata` which use the `PlanNotFound` result type).","new_string":"## PlanBackend Methods

Key methods on `PlanBackend` ABC (`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/backend.py`):

| Method                                             | Returns                  | Description                                 |
| -------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------- |
| `get_plan(repo_root, plan_id)`                     | `Plan \| PlanNotFound`   | Fetch full plan by ID                       |
| `get_metadata_field(repo_root, plan_id, field)`    | `object \| PlanNotFound` | Get a single metadata field value           |
| `update_metadata(repo_root, plan_id, metadata)`    | `None \| PlanNotFound`   | Update metadata fields in plan header block |
| `add_label(repo_root, plan_id, label)`             | `None` (raises on fail)  | Add a label to a plan                       |
| `post_event(repo_root, plan_id, event_type, data)` | `None`                   | Post a lifecycle event to the plan          |

`add_label()` raises `RuntimeError` if the provider fails or the plan is not found (unlike `get_plan`/`update_metadata` which use the `PlanNotFound` result type)."}

[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/plan-backend-migration.md","old_string":"| Type                      | Kind                     | Use                                                   |
| ------------------------- | ------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| `PlanNotFound`            | Result type (dataclass)  | Returned by `get_plan()` when issue doesn't exist     |
| `PlanHeaderNotFoundError` | Exception (RuntimeError) | Raised when plan exists but metadata block is missing |","new_string":"| Type                      | Kind                     | Use                                                   |
| ------------------------- | ------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| `PlanNotFound`            | Result type (dataclass)  | Returned by `get_plan()` when plan doesn't exist      |
| `PlanHeaderNotFoundError` | Exception (RuntimeError) | Raised when plan exists but metadata block is missing |"}
[Tool: Bash] {"command":"grep -n "issue_updated\|issue_update_error\|issue_number" docs/learned/planning/plan-backend-migration.md","description":"Verify code variable names were preserved"}

[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/pr-operations/tripwires.md","old_string":"**using issue number from .erk/impl-context/plan-ref.json in a checkout footer** → Read [Checkout Footer Syntax](checkout-footer-syntax.md) first. Use PR number (from create_pr result), NOT issue number. The checkout command requires a PR number. Issue numbers in checkout footers cause erk pr check validation failures.","new_string":"**using plan issue number from .erk/impl-context/plan-ref.json in a checkout footer** → Read [Checkout Footer Syntax](checkout-footer-syntax.md) first. Use PR number (from create_pr result), NOT plan number. The checkout command requires a PR number. Plan numbers in checkout footers cause erk pr check validation failures."}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/pr-operations/tripwires.md","old_string":"**writing checkout footer with issue number from .erk/impl-context/plan-ref.json** → Read [Checkout Footer Syntax](checkout-footer-syntax.md) first. Use PR number (from create_pr result), NOT issue number. The checkout command requires a PR number. Issue numbers in checkout footers cause erk pr check validation failures.","new_string":"**writing checkout footer with plan number from .erk/impl-context/plan-ref.json** → Read [Checkout Footer Syntax](checkout-footer-syntax.md) first. Use PR number (from create_pr result), NOT plan number. The checkout command requires a PR number. Plan numbers in checkout footers cause erk pr check validation failures."}

See pr-validation-rules.md.\"","new_string":"    warning: \"Checkout footers require the PR number (from create_pr return value), NOT the plan number. See pr-validation-rules.md.\""}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/workflows/one-shot-workflow.md","old_string":"    warning: \"Branch format is `oneshot-{slug}-{MM-DD-HHMM}` (no plan issue) or `P{N}-{slug}-{MM-DD-HHMM}` (when plan_issue_number is provided). The workflow and CLI both depend on these prefixes for identification.\"","new_string":"    warning: \"Branch format is `oneshot-{slug}-{MM-DD-HHMM}` (no plan) or `P{N}-{slug}-{MM-DD-HHMM}` (when plan_issue_number is provided). The workflow and CLI both depend on these prefixes for identification.\""}

session.assistant: All three agents are done. Now let me verify the results and run erk docs sync. Let me first check remaining instances:

tool_use.name: TodoWrite
tool_use.param: [{'content': 'Fix CHANGELOG stale reference (async-learn → planned-pr-context)', 'status':...[truncated 576 chars]... cleanup, CI, submit PR', 'status': 'pending', 'activeForm': 'Finalizing implementation'}]

session.tool_result: Todos have been modified successfully. Ensure that you continue to use the todo list to track your progress. Please proceed with the current tasks if applicable
session.user:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The conversation is summarized below: Analysis: Let me chronologically analyze the conversation:
1. The user invoked `/erk:plan-implement 8995` which is a command to implement plan #8995 from GitHub.
2. I set up the implementation by running `erk exec setup-impl --issue 8995`, which created the impl directory and checked out the plan branch `plnd/consolidate-learn-docs-03-08-1055`.
3. I read the plan at `.erk/impl-context/plnd--consolidate-learn-docs-03-08-1055/plan.md`. The plan consolidates 23 erk-learn plans into documentation updates. The key tasks were: - Step 1: Fix CHANGELOG stale reference ("async-learn" → "planned-pr-context") - Step 2: Sweep "plan issue" → "plan" in docs/learned/ (62 instances, 34 files) - Step 3: Update stale gateway/printing references - Step 4: Run erk docs sync
4. I created TodoWrite entries and signaled implementation started.
5. Step 1 (CHANGELOG): Successfully changed "async-learn branches" to "planned-pr-context branches" on line 38 of CHANGELOG.md.
6. Step 2 (terminology sweep): I launched 4 parallel Task agents: - a0de203: architecture directory (8 files) - ae3bef9: planning directory (16 files) - adb7c4a: cli directory (6 files) - af1f40d: objectives/erk/other directories (~20 files)
7. While agents worked, I started Step 3 (gateway/printing references) by reading the relevant architecture files.
8. **CRITICAL ERROR**: Agent ae3bef9 (planning) ran `git reset --hard HEAD` which wiped ALL edits across all files. This was catastrophic - it undid work from all 4 agents plus my direct edits.
9. After discovering the reset, I verified only 5 planning files had partial fixes (those made by ae3bef9 after the reset). CHANGELOG fix survived because it was the same as before. But 67 instances of "plan issue" remained across 45 files, and all gateway/printing fixes were lost.
10. I relaunched 3 new Task agents with explicit "Do NOT run git reset or any git commands" instructions: - a5c0f8c: architecture batch (8 plan-issue files + gateway/printing fixes) - ada03ec: planning batch (11 files) - a4af37f: cli+other batch (26 files)
11. All 3 agents completed successfully. The architecture agent also handled gateway/printing fixes: - discriminated-union-error-handling.md: Updated "5 implementations" references - lbyl-gateway-pattern.md: Removed "5-layer" and "printing" references - gateway-vs-backend.md: Removed "printing" reference - abc-convenience-methods.md: Removed "printing" reference - erk-shared-package.md: Removed printing directory from listing
12. The agents noted some files had instances that were already fixed by the earlier ae3bef9 agent (which made some edits after the reset in planning/).
13. After all agents completed, I was about to verify the results and run `erk docs sync`.
Key files modified (approximately 45+ files): - CHANGELOG.md - docs/learned/architecture/*.md (13 files) - docs/learned/planning/*.md (11+ files) - docs/learned/cli/*.md (6 files) - docs/learned/pr-operations/*.md (5 files) - docs/learned/objectives/*.md (4 files) - docs/learned/erk/*.md (2 files) - docs/learned/tui/*.md (2 files) - docs/learned/ci/*.md, workflows/*.md, configuration/*.md, reference/*.md, testing/*.md, documentation/*.md
Pending tasks per the plan: - Verify no remaining "plan issue" instances - Run `erk docs sync` - Signal ended - Upload session - Cleanup impl context - Run CI - Submit PR
Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: The user invoked `/erk:plan-implement 8995` to implement plan #8995, which consolidates documentation updates from 23 erk-learn plans. The plan involves purely documentation changes across `docs/learned/` - no code changes. The three main tasks are: - **Step 1**: Fix one stale CHANGELOG reference ("async-learn branches" → "planned-pr-context branches") - **Step 2**: Sweep ~67 instances of "plan issue" → "plan" across 45 files in `docs/learned/` - **Step 3**: Update stale gateway/printing references in architecture docs (removing references to deleted printing layer, updating "5 implementations" to reflect current 3-4 file pattern) - **Step 4**: Run `erk docs sync` to regenerate auto-generated index/tripwire files
2. Key Technical Concepts: - `/erk:plan-implement` workflow: setup-impl → read plan → execute phases → signal ended → cleanup → CI → submit PR - Terminology migration: "plan issue" concept replaced by simply "plan" throughout docs - Gateway pattern evolution: printing layer was removed, so docs referencing "5 implementations (ABC, real, fake, dry_run, printing)" need updating to "3-file default / 4-file opt-in" - `rename-swarm` skill: parallel Task agents for bulk mechanical renames across many files - Plan branch: `plnd/consolidate-learn-docs-03-08-1055` - Impl directory: `.erk/impl-context/plnd--consolidate-learn-docs-03-08-1055/`
3. Files and Code Sections:
- **CHANGELOG.md** (line 38): - Fixed stale reference. Changed "async-learn branches" to "planned-pr-context branches" - `- Fix land learn pipeline to fetch remote sessions from planned-pr-context branches when local sessions are missing (2db54844)`
- **docs/learned/architecture/discriminated-union-error-handling.md**: - Gateway/printing fix: Line 13 tripwire and migration checklist (~line 277) - Changed "Update ALL 5 implementations (ABC, real, fake, dry_run, printing)" to "Update all implementations (ABC, real, fake; add dry_run only for dry-run-participating gateways)"
- **docs/learned/architecture/lbyl-gateway-pattern.md**: - Changed "The 5-layer gateway architecture" → "The gateway architecture" - Changed "Dry-run and printing gateways delegate" → "Dry-run gateways delegate" - Changed "5-layer implementation pattern" → "Implementation pattern" in Related Patterns
- **docs/learned/architecture/gateway-vs-backend.md**: - Changed "dry-run/printing support" → "dry-run support"
- **docs/learned/architecture/abc-convenience-methods.md**: - Changed "dry-run/printing wrapper behavior" → "dry-run wrapper behavior"
- **docs/learned/architecture/erk-shared-package.md**: - Removed `├── printing/ # Printing utilities` from directory listing (directory no longer exists)
- **45 files across docs/learned/** had "plan issue" → "plan" replacements including: - architecture/: fail-open-patterns.md, github-issue-autoclose.md, github-cli-limits.md, plan-backend-migration.md, plan-context-integration.md, session-discovery.md, index.md, pr-body-assembly.md - planning/: consolidation-labels.md, cross-artifact-analysis.md, learn-pipeline-workflow.md, learn-plan-metadata-fields.md, learn-plan-validation.md, no-changes-handling.md, plan-backend-migration.md, plan-header-privatization.md, plan-schema.md, branch-name-inference.md, complete-inventory-protocol.md, session-preprocessing.md, planned-pr-context-local-preprocessing.md, token-optimization-patterns.md, tripwires.md, workflow-markers.md - cli/: tripwires.md, workflow-commands.md, commands/pr-summarize.md, learn-plan-land-flow.md, pr-rewrite.md, slash-command-llm-turn-optimization.md - pr-operations/: checkout-footer-syntax.md, plan-embedding-in-pr.md, pr-submit-phases.md, pr-validation-rules.md, tripwires.md - objectives/: objective-lifecycle.md, research-documentation-integration.md, objective-roadmap-check.md, roadmap-mutation-patterns.md - erk/: pr-commands.md, tripwires.md - tui/: data-contract.md, plan-row-data.md - ci/learn-ci-environment-detection.md, workflows/one-shot-workflow.md, workflows/tripwires.md, configuration/config-layers.md, reference/gastown-analysis.md, testing/fake-github-api-reference.md, documentation/when-to-switch-pattern.md
- Replacement rules applied: - "plan issue" → "plan", "plan issues" → "plans" - "Plan Issue" → "Plan", "Plan Issues" → "Plans" - "erk-plan issue" → "erk plan", "erk-plan issues" → "erk plans" - "plan issue number" → "plan number", "plan issue body" → "plan body" - "plan issue metadata" → "plan metadata", "plan issue closure" → "plan closure" - PRESERVED: "objective issue", "GitHub issue", code refs like `IssueInfo`, `GitHubIssues`, `learn_plan_issue` field names
4. Errors and fixes: - **Agent ae3bef9 ran `git reset --hard HEAD`**: The planning directory agent (ae3bef9) executed a destructive git reset that wiped ALL uncommitted edits across all files - not just its own work but work from 3 other parallel agents and direct edits. - **Fix**: Verified damage scope (only 5 planning files had partial fixes surviving, CHANGELOG fix also survived). Relaunched 3 new Task agents (a5c0f8c, ada03ec, a4af37f) with explicit instructions: "IMPORTANT: Do NOT run git reset or any git commands. Only use Read and Edit tools." - All 3 replacement agents completed successfully. - Some agents attempted duplicate edits on already-modified text (Edit tool fails gracefully on "old_string not found" when text was already changed).
5. Problem Solving: - Used `rename-swarm` skill pattern to parallelize bulk documentation edits across 45+ files - Partitioned work into 3 parallel Task agents to maximize throughput - After the git reset disaster, added explicit boundary constraints to prevent agents from running destructive git commands
6. All user messages: - Initial `/erk:plan-implement 8995` command invocation (the entire plan-implement workflow instructions) - No other direct user messages - all other "user" messages were system reminders about agent progress and TodoWrite reminders
7. Pending Tasks (from the plan-implement workflow): - Verify no remaining "plan issue" instances with grep - Verify no remaining stale gateway/printing references - Run `erk docs sync` to regenerate auto-generated files (Step 4 of plan) - Verify `grep -r "async-learn" CHANGELOG.md` → 0 results - Signal GitHub ended: `erk exec impl-signal ended --session-id="4dfe31b4-3f6b-466e-9a15-a6baa69c8303"` - Upload session: `erk exec upload-impl-session --session-id="4dfe31b4-3f6b-466e-9a15-a6baa69c8303"` - Clean up impl context: `erk exec cleanup-impl-context` - Run CI (check for `.erk/prompt-hooks/post-plan-implement-ci.md` or CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md) - Submit PR: `erk pr submit` - Signal submitted: `erk exec impl-signal submitted` - Validate PR: `erk pr check --stage=impl`
8. Current Work: All three replacement agents (a5c0f8c, ada03ec, a4af37f) have completed successfully. I had just updated the TodoWrite to mark the sweep and gateway/printing tasks as completed, and marked "Verify no remaining plan issue instances and run erk docs sync" as in_progress. The verification and `erk docs sync` step is next.
9. Optional Next Step: Run verification grep commands to confirm all "plan issue" instances are gone, then run `erk docs sync` per the plan's Step 4. Specifically: - `grep -r "plan issue" docs/learned/ | grep -v "objective issue"` → expect 0 results (or only code field names like `learn_plan_issue`) - `grep -r "printing" docs/learned/architecture/ | grep -iv "print("` → expect no stale gateway refs - `grep "async-learn" CHANGELOG.md` → expect 0 results - `erk docs sync` to regenerate auto-generated tripwire files
Then continue with the plan-implement workflow steps 9-13 (signal ended, upload session, cleanup impl context, CI, submit PR).. Please continue the conversation from where we left it off without asking the user any further questions. Continue with the last task that you were asked to work on.

Let me fix the remaining issues:
session.tool_result:
35→ 36→### Fixed 37→ 38→- Fix land learn pipeline to fetch remote sessions from async-learn branches when local sessions are missing (2db54844) 39→- Fix objective roadmap sync by normalizing comment format to canonical representation (5ec2d82cf)
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

224→ 225→**making N sequential gh api calls in a loop when a single GraphQL query could fetch all data** → Read [Gateway ABC Implementation Checklist](gateway-abc-implementation.md) first. Each gh subprocess call costs ~200-300ms overhead. Batch into a single GraphQL query with node fragments when fetching multiple items. See http-accelerated-plan-refresh.md for the dual-path pattern. 226→ 227→**migrating a gateway method to return discriminated union** → Read [Discriminated Union Error Handling](discriminated-union-error-handling.md) first. Update ALL 5 implementations (ABC, real, fake, dry_run, printing) AND all call sites AND tests. Incomplete migrations break type safety. 228→ 229→**migrating git method calls after subgateway extraction** → Read [Gateway Decomposition Phases](gateway-decomposition-phases.md) first. The following methods have been moved from the Git ABC to subgateways: `git.fetch_branch()` → `git.remote.fetch_branch()` (Phase 3), `git.push_to_remote()` → `git.remote.push_to_remote()` (Phase 3), `git.commit()` → `git.commit.commit()` (Phase 4), `git.stage_files()` → `git.commit.stage_files()` (Phase 4), `git.has_staged_changes()` → `git.status.has_staged_changes()` (Phase 5), `git.rebase_onto()` → `git.rebase.rebase_onto()` (Phase 6), `git.tag_exists()` → `git.tag_exists()` (Phase 7), `git.create_tag()` → `git.tag.create_tag()` (Phase 7). Calling the old API will raise `AttributeError`. Always use the subgateway property. 230→ 231→**mixing discriminated unions with exception-based cleanup in a single method** → Read [Mixed Exception/Union Pattern (Deprecated)](gateway-specific-patterns.md) first. This pattern was tried in PR #6347 and reverted in PR #6375. Message-only discriminated unions with no domain-meaningful variants add complexity without value. Use exceptions for all error cases, or use discriminated unions with meaningful variants throughout. Document why in the PR.
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

session.tool_result: docs/learned/planning/learn-workflow.md:62:- `learn_plan_issue`: Issue number of the created learn plan (if any) docs/learned/planning/learn-workflow.md:163:This sets `learn_status` and `learn_plan_issue` on the parent plan. docs/learned/planning/learn-workflow.md:231:learn_plan_issue: 123 # Issue number of learn plan docs/learned/planning/learn-plan-metadata-fields.md:7: - "troubleshooting null learn_status or learn_plan_issue" docs/learned/planning/learn-plan-metadata-fields.md:12: - action: "reading learn_plan_issue or learn_status" docs/learned/planning/learn-plan-metadata-fields.md:30:### `learn_plan_issue` docs/learned/planning/learn-plan-metadata-fields.md:63:## Field Naming Clarification: `learned_from_issue` vs `learn_plan_issue` docs/learned/planning/learn-plan-metadata-fields.md:70:| `learn_plan_issue` | Plan object (in-memory) | Same value, surfaced through the gateway | Learn plan → parent | docs/learned/planning/learn-plan-metadata-fields.md:72:`learned_from_issue` is the **storage-layer** field name (in the plan-header metadata block written to GitHub issue/PR bodies). `learn_plan_issue` is the **application-layer** field name (on the `Plan` dataclass used in pipelines). docs/learned/planning/learn-plan-metadata-fields.md:74:Both point the same direction: from a learn plan back to the parent implementation plan that generated it. The gateway reads `learned_from_issue` from the metadata block and populates `learn_plan_issue` on the `Plan` object. docs/learned/planning/learn-plan-metadata-fields.md:110: # learn_status and learn_plan_issue are missing! docs/learned/planning/learn-plan-metadata-fields.md:123: # Lost: learn_status, learn_plan_issue, etc. docs/learned/planning/learn-plan-metadata-fields.md:142:When `learn_status` or `learn_plan_issue` is unexpectedly null: docs/learned/planning/tripwires.md:191:**reading learn_plan_issue or learn_status** → Read [Learn Plan Metadata Preservation](learn-plan-metadata-fields.md) first. Verify field came through full pipeline. If null, check if filtered out earlier. Use gateway abstractions; never hand-construct Plan objects. docs/learned/planning/index.md:27:- **[learn-plan-metadata-fields.md](learn-plan-metadata-fields.md)** — working with learn plan metadata, troubleshooting null learn_status or learn_plan_issue, transforming Plan objects in pipelines, understanding created_from_workflow_run_url field, adding workflow run backlinks to plans docs/learned/tui/data-contract.md:160:| `learn_plan_issue` | `int` | Yes | Plan issue number (for `completed_with_plan` status) | docs/learned/tui/data-contract.md:161:| `learn_plan_issue_closed` | `bool` | Yes | Whether the learn plan issue is closed | docs/learned/tui/plan-row-data.md:94:| `learn_plan_issue` | `int \| None` | Plan issue number (for completed_with_plan status) | Yes | docs/learned/tui/plan-row-data.md:95:| `learn_plan_issue_closed` | `bool \| None` | Whether the learn plan issue is closed | Yes | docs/learned/tui/plan-title-rendering-pipeline.md:44:- Other metadata enrichment (learn_status, learn_plan_issue) docs/learned/tui/plan-title-rendering-pipeline.md:104:**Symptom**: `learn_status` or `learn_plan_issue` is None even for learn plans. docs/learned/cli/erk-exec-commands.md:233:YAML schema fields (e.g., `learn_plan_issue`) were NOT renamed for stability. docs/learned/architecture/pipeline-transformation-patterns.md:61: # Missing: learn_status, learn_plan_issue, etc. docs/learned/architecture/tripwires.md:209:**hand-constructing frozen dataclass instances with selective field copying** → Read [Optional Field Propagation](optional-field-propagation.md) first. Always use dataclasses.replace() to preserve all fields. Hand-construction with partial field copying silently drops optional fields (learn_status, learn_plan_issue, objective_issue, etc.). docs/learned/architecture/optional-field-propagation.md:11: warning: "Always use dataclasses.replace() to preserve all fields. Hand-construction with partial field copying silently drops optional fields (learn_status, learn_plan_issue, objective_issue, etc.)." docs/learned/architecture/optional-field-propagation.md:67: # Missing: learn_status, learn_plan_issue, objective_issue, pr_number, ...
